message:
perf(types,core): vectorized batch MakeRecord encoder

Add `fsqlite_types::record::encode_batch(rows, out, offsets)` — a
MonetDB/X100-style two-pass batch encoder for the SQLite record format:

- Pass 1 measures per-row (header, body) sizes and computes the total
  output size. Stored in a column-oriented scratch Vec.
- Pass 2 resizes `out` once to the total size, then streams header
  varints + payload bytes per row. `offsets[i]` records row i's start.

Byte-identical guarantee: for any row r, `out[offsets[i]..end]` equals
exactly what scalar `serialize_record(&rows[i])` produces. Enforced by
proptest (100 random batches) + 5 targeted unit tests (empty, single,
heterogeneous, 1000-row stress, capacity preservation).

Wired into the multi-row INSERT path in
`crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs`: when the path already
materializes multiple rows with patched rowid substitution, the
vectorized path avoids per-row Vec growth from `resize` and keeps the
encoder loop streaming into a pre-sized destination. Gated behind
`PRAGMA fsqlite.vectorized_makerecord = SAFE | OFF` (default SAFE /
ON because output is byte-identical; OFF preserved for regression
bisection).

Target: wide-row INSERT (large_10col 100K currently 9.4x slower than
C SQLite). Expected 20-30% reduction per AG-3A proposal; actual bench
delta to be measured in post-campaign run.

Salvaged from worktree-agent-a470c11f which hit usage limit before
benchmarking. Compile clean; all 6 record::tests::encode_batch_* pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
